channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Modiphius Entertainment Brings Facist Murder to Table With Wolfenstein: The Roleplaying Game
item.content:encoded:
Modiphius has announced an official TTRPG based on an iconic videogame property. Wolfenstein: The Roleplaying Game aims to bring the fascist-killing FPS franchise to gaming tables.

According to a press release, Wolfenstein: The Roleplaying Game will use Modiphius' 2d20 RPG system. This is the same system that has powered other licensed RPGs in their catalog like Star Trek Adventures and the Fallout RPG.

For much older gamers, it may appear that the project doesn't have a lot of compelling material. Wolfenstein was a PC gaming pioneer with Wolfenstein 3D being one of the first FPS games ever. It was also decidedly light on story. Your character, BJ Blazkowicz, is on a mission to stop a Nazi operation in Castle Wolfenstein. Cue a bunch of gunfights; roll credits.

But Wolfenstein: The Roleplaying Game will be focusing on the more recent games developed by Machine Games. These critically acclaimed titles, Wolfenstein: The New Order and Wolfenstein: The New Colossus, puts BJ Blazkowicz in a horrifying alternate history. One where the Nazis won WW2 and have taken over the world.

These titles have a lot going for them. The setting is a heady mix of dieselpunk technology and alt 60s pop culture. BJ is given strong characterization and a compelling support group of freedom fighters, making him more than a blank slate videogame protagonist. Lastly, the games' have strong central messages of resistance that go beyond the surface.

Players in this TTRPG are a part of that resistance. The game will include rules for advanced weaponry and technology, allowing characters to fight against their oppressors with blood, fire, and fury.

Chris Birch, co-founder of Modiphius, said, “As a result of our continued relationship with Bethesda Softworks, we’re thrilled to announce that we are working on a tabletop roleplaying game for Wolfenstein. We hope that fans will enjoy being able to immerse themselves in the world in a new way. Get psyched!”

The plan for Wolfenstein: The Roleplaying Game is to crowdfund the title in Fall of 2026. At time of writing, details are sparse regarding which platform or what incentives will be included.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler
